cards.front: How many Kandas (books) are in the Valmiki Ramayan?
cards.back: Seven: Bala, Ayodhya, Aranya, Kishkindha, Sundara, Yuddha, and Uttara Kanda.

cards.front: Who is the author of the Ramayan, and what is his title?
cards.back: Sage Valmiki, revered as the Adi Kavi (the first poet); the Ramayan is the Adi Kavya.

cards.front: Roughly how many shlokas does the Valmiki Ramayan contain?
cards.back: About 24,000 shlokas.

cards.front: What event inspired Valmiki's very first shloka?
cards.back: Seeing a hunter kill one of a pair of krauncha (curlew) birds; his grief became the verse 'Ma Nishada'.

cards.front: Who is Rama's father?
cards.back: King Dasharatha of Ayodhya.

cards.front: Who is Rama's mother?
cards.back: Queen Kausalya.

cards.front: Name Dasharatha's three queens.
cards.back: Kausalya, Kaikeyi, and Sumitra.

cards.front: Who is Bharata's mother?
cards.back: Queen Kaikeyi.

cards.front: Who is the mother of Lakshmana and Shatrughna?
cards.back: Queen Sumitra.

cards.front: Who is Sita's father, and which kingdom does he rule?
cards.back: King Janaka of Mithila (Videha); Sita is also called Janaki and Vaidehi.

cards.front: Who are Hanuman's divine father and mother?
cards.back: Vayu (the wind god) is his spiritual father; his mother is Anjana.

cards.front: Who is Sugriva's estranged brother?
cards.back: Vali (Bali), king of Kishkindha.

cards.front: Name Ravana's two famous brothers.
cards.back: Kumbhakarna (the great sleeper) and Vibhishana (the righteous one who joins Rama).

cards.front: Who is Ravana's mighty son, master of illusions?
cards.back: Indrajit, also called Meghnad.

cards.front: Who is Ravana's chief queen?
cards.back: Mandodari.

cards.front: Why is Ravana called Dashanana?
cards.back: Because he has ten heads (dasha = ten, anana = face).

cards.front: Which vulture king fights Ravana to save Sita?
cards.back: Jatayu; his brother is Sampati.

cards.front: Who is Vali's wife, and Angada's mother?
cards.back: Tara, renowned for her wisdom.

cards.front: Which sage takes the young Rama and Lakshmana to protect his sacrifice?
cards.back: Sage Vishwamitra.

cards.front: Which demoness does Rama slay as a boy at Vishwamitra's request?
cards.back: Tataka.

cards.front: Whom does Rama liberate from a curse by the touch of his feet?
cards.back: Ahalya, wife of sage Gautama, who had been turned to stone.

cards.front: How does Rama win Sita's hand at the swayamvara?
cards.back: By lifting and breaking the divine bow of Shiva (Pinaka) in Janaka's court.

cards.front: How long is Rama's exile, and why?
cards.back: Fourteen years in the forest, to honour Dasharatha's boon to Kaikeyi.

cards.front: What two boons does Kaikeyi demand?
cards.back: That Bharata be crowned king, and that Rama be exiled to the forest for fourteen years.

cards.front: What does Bharata place on the throne in Rama's absence?
cards.back: Rama's sandals (paduka), ruling as his regent from Nandigrama.

cards.front: Which Nishada (boatman) chief is Rama's devoted friend on the journey?
cards.back: Guha, king of the Nishadas.

cards.front: Which demoness is disfigured for her advances, sparking the war?
cards.back: Surpanakha, Ravana's sister; Lakshmana cuts off her nose and ears.

cards.front: Who takes the form of the golden deer to lure Rama away?
cards.back: The demon Mareecha.

cards.front: What is the 'Lakshmana Rekha'?
cards.back: The protective line Lakshmana draws around Sita before leaving; she crosses it and is abducted.

cards.front: Which elderly devotee offers Rama tasted berries with pure love?
cards.back: Shabari.

cards.front: How does Rama help Sugriva regain his kingdom?
cards.back: By slaying Vali, restoring Sugriva to the throne of Kishkindha.

cards.front: Which dejected vanara does Hanuman inspire to leap the ocean?
cards.back: Hanuman himself leaps, encouraged by Jambavan who reminds him of his powers.

cards.front: What is the Sundara Kanda chiefly about?
cards.back: Hanuman's leap to Lanka, finding Sita in the Ashoka grove, and burning Lanka.

cards.front: What token does Rama give Hanuman to prove his identity to Sita?
cards.back: Rama's signet ring (anguliya); Sita sends back her chudamani (crest jewel).

cards.front: How does Hanuman set Lanka ablaze?
cards.back: Ravana sets fire to Hanuman's tail; Hanuman uses it to burn the city.

cards.front: Who builds the bridge (Setu) to Lanka, and with whose help?
cards.back: The vanara architect Nala, with the army of monkeys; it is called Rama Setu.

cards.front: Which herb does Hanuman fetch to revive Lakshmana?
cards.back: The Sanjeevani herb; unable to identify it, he carries the whole Dronagiri mountain.

cards.front: Who reveals the secret of Ravana's death to Rama?
cards.back: Vibhishana, Ravana's righteous brother who defected to Rama.

cards.front: How is Ravana finally slain?
cards.back: By Rama's Brahmastra arrow piercing the nectar in his navel.

cards.front: What trial does Sita undergo after the war?
cards.back: The Agni Pariksha (trial by fire) to prove her purity.

cards.front: What festival celebrates Rama's return to Ayodhya?
cards.back: Diwali, marking his homecoming and coronation after fourteen years.

cards.front: Who are Lava and Kusha?
cards.back: The twin sons of Rama and Sita, raised in Valmiki's hermitage, who recite the Ramayan.

cards.front: Recite the first shloka of the Ramayan (Ma Nishada).
cards.back: मा निषाद प्रतिष्ठां त्वमगमः शाश्वतीः समाः। यत्क्रौञ्चमिथुनादेकमवधीः काममोहितम्॥ — 'O hunter, may you never find peace, for you killed one of a pair of birds lost in love.'

cards.front: What does the name 'Rama' signify?
cards.back: 'One who delights' / the one in whom all beings rejoice (from the root ram, to delight).

cards.front: Complete: 'Rama Rama Rameti...'
cards.back: रामे रामे रमे रामे, मनोरमे सहस्रनाम तत्तुल्यं राम नाम वरानने — chanting 'Rama' thrice equals the thousand names of Vishnu.
